page.content: The data table presents an analysis of auditory function outcomes following cell transplantation, specifically focusing on average pre-operative air-bone gap (ABG) measurements across different patient groups. The table distinguishes between two cohorts: TEES (n = 53) and MES (n = 100), providing a comparative overview of initial cases and subsequent improvements in hearing capabilities post-transplantation. Notably, the average pre-operative ABG ± SD (dB) for the TEES cohort is recorded at 24.7 ± 8.1, while the MES cohort exhibits a slightly higher average of 25.0 ± 8.2.

page.content: This subtle difference underscores the variability in auditory impairment severity prior to intervention. Additionally, the column detailing the number of cases improved 30 days after cell transplantation (n) indicates significant outcomes in both groups, reflecting the potential efficacy of the procedure. Overall, these metrics not only highlight the initial auditory challenges faced by patients but also set the stage for evaluating the long-term benefits of cell transplantation in enhancing hearing functions. The years 2012 to 2015 suggest a longitudinal perspective on these outcomes, enhancing the relevance of the findings.
